title: coleus thalassoscopicus
intro: Coleus thalassoscopicus, a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia, is a member of the Lamiaceae family and is commonly known as the blue water coleus or blue water geranium. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Coleus thalassoscopicus include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Coleus thalassoscopicus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to create medicinal products, such as capsules and ointments.
